metadata.description: 异步编程: Async/await coroutines are a model for single-threaded concurrency, suitable for high I/O concurrency. Here is a brief Python code example to illustrate the use of `async` and `await`: ```python import asyncio # Define an asynchronous function using async/await async def fetch_data(): print("Start fetching") await asyncio.sleep(2) # Simulate an I/O operation print("Data fetched") return {"data": 123} # Main function to run the coroutine async def main(): result = await fetch_data() # Wait for the coroutine to complete print(result) # Run the event loop asyncio.run(main()) ``` In this example, `fetch_data` is an asynchronous function that uses `await` to wait for an I/O operation (simulated by `asyncio.sleep(2)`). The `main` function demonstrates how to call and await the completion of an async function.
